title: iria retusa
intro: Iria retusa, also known as the bitter tree or bitter bark, is a tropical plant native to the Caribbean and Central America. The plant's bark and roots are used medicinally due to their rich content of alkaloids, terpenes, and flavonoids, which possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Iria retusa has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation, and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, or used in traditional medicine to leverage its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health ailments.
